nativeRange.habitat: Deep coastal to outer reef drop-offs and steep slopes; tends to occur over rubble or sandy bottoms with small isolated patch reefs of low profile. Adults have been documented at depths from roughly 25–52 m, with juveniles sometimes found shallower.

diet.notes: Tends to accept a variety of meaty foods including frozen mysis shrimp, brine shrimp, and other small crustacean-based preparations. Like most fairy wrasses, it is generally considered an eager feeder once established and has been reported to readily adapt to prepared and frozen foods.

avoidWith: large predatory fish that may consume small fish
other male Cirrhilabrus in small tanks

summary: A stunning reef-safe fairy wrasse from the Western Pacific, known for its pink-to-red margined fins and vivid coloration in males. Generally considered an intermediate-level species that tends to thrive in peaceful reef communities with a tight-fitting lid.
careNotes:
Pink Margin Fairy Wrasse (Cirrhilabrus rubrimarginatus) is a vibrant, moderately-sized fairy wrasse naturally occurring across the Western Pacific from the Ryukyu Islands through the Philippines, Palau, Indonesia, Vanuatu, Fiji, and Tonga. In the wild it tends to inhabit deeper reef drop-offs and rubble zones, generally at 25–52 m depth, though juveniles have been documented in shallower waters.

Males display dramatically more intense coloration than females, with vivid pink to red fin margins; females are less colorful and generally smaller. This species is a protogynous hermaphrodite (female-first sex changer), meaning females can transition to terminal-phase males under appropriate social conditions. It is commonly kept as a harem — one male with one or more females — or as a mated pair. Keeping two males together in a smaller aquarium is generally inadvisable due to conspecific aggression between males.

A tightly-sealed aquarium lid with no large openings is strongly advised: wrasses in general and fairy wrasses in particular are well-documented jumpers that can escape through small gaps. Temperature is generally advised to be maintained in the 23.8–28.5°C range based on FishBase wild data. BlueZoo Aquatics recommends a minimum 50-gallon (approximately 189 L) tank; 200 L is suggested here as a conservative minimum that provides adequate open swimming space for an active mid-water swimmer.

Also sold as the 'Red Margin Fairy Wrasse'. Male specimens tend to command a premium over females in the trade.
breedingNotes: Breeding in captivity is rarely documented for this species. FishBase notes distinct pairing during breeding in the wild, consistent with the harem social structure typical of Cirrhilabrus wrasses. Protogynous sex change means that in a group without a dominant male, the largest/most dominant female may eventually transition to male phase.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Collection page confirmed species presence in reef-safe wrasse category; direct product page not accessible during research. Species care data sourced primarily from BlueZoo Aquatics and FishBase.

additional.name: BlueZoo Aquatics
additional.notes: Confirmed: mature size 5 inches (12.7 cm), minimum tank 50 gallons, care level intermediate, temperament peaceful, reef compatible yes, diet carnivore, native range Indo-Pacific/Fiji/Tonga. Also confirmed escape-risk warning: 'Wrasses are known to jump from aquariums. Be sure to have some sort of covering with no large open holes that they can escape from.' Listed as both Pink Margin Fairy Wrasse and Red Margin Fairy Wrasse.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Confirmed: max size 12.2 cm SL, temperature range 23.8–28.5°C (mean 26.5°C), family Labridae, native range Western Pacific including Ryukyu Islands/Philippines/Palau/Indonesia/Vanuatu/Fiji/Tonga, depth 25–52 m, habitat deep reef drop-offs and rubble, distinct pairing during breeding. Cross-checked size (12.2 vs 12.7 cm from BlueZoo — minor 0.5 cm spread, consistent). Temperature data from FishBase only (BlueZoo did not specify water parameters).

fish.breedingNotes: Rarely bred in captivity. Protogynous hermaphrodite that forms harems in the wild. Captive spawning has been attempted but successful rearing of larvae is largely undocumented for this species.
